results.title: PTO Carryover Rules - Labor Law Reference
results.snippet: Employers may limit annual PTO carryover; many state laws permit a cap of up to 5 days, though specific limits vary by jurisdiction.

pages.title: PTO Carryover Rules - Labor Law Reference
pages.content: Under current labor regulations, employers are generally permitted to set reasonable caps on annual paid-time-off (PTO) carryover. A common statutory safe harbor allows a maximum of 5 days (40 hours) of unused PTO to be carried into the following calendar year. Employers may adopt more generous policies, but they must apply these consistently across all eligible employees. Any PTO beyond this 5-day limit may be forfeited if the employer’s written policy clearly states the cap. This provision does not require payout of forfeited days unless state law mandates it.
